query: NORAD 68369 Rassvet-3 No 10 current orbital elements perigee June 2026
answer:
Now I have a comprehensive picture. Let me compile the final research report.

---

## NORAD 68369 — RASSVET-3 No 10 (2026-061K): Current Orbital Elements & Perigee, June 2026

### Satellite Identity

| Parameter | Value |
|---|---|
| **NORAD Catalog ID** | 68369 |
| **International Designator** | 2026-061K |
| **Name** | RASSVET-3 10 (Object 10) |
| **Operator** | Byuro 1440 (Bureau 1440) |
| **Mass** | ~370 kg |
| **Type** | LEO broadband communication satellite |
| **Launch Date** | 2026-03-23 17:24 UTC |
| **Launch Vehicle** | Soyuz-2.1b with Fregat-M upper stage |
| **Launch Site** | Plesetsk Cosmodrome, LC-43/4, Russia |
| **Status** | Operational (in orbit, maintaining altitude) |

### Most Current TLE (Epoch: June 14, 2026 22:37:40 UTC)

```
1 68369U 26061K   26165.94282494  .00116972  41251-5  25961-3 0  9999
2 68369  82.2897  14.0549 0009892 144.2136 215.9805 15.97751051 13243
```

**Source:** N2YO.com / AFSPC (accessed June 15, 2026)

### Derived Orbital Elements (from June 14 TLE)

| Element | Value |
|---|---|
| **Perigee altitude** | **~274 km** |
| **Apogee altitude** | **~287 km** |
| **Semi-major axis** | 6,658.8 km |
| **Eccentricity** | 0.0009892 |
| **Inclination** | 82.2897° |
| **RAAN** | 14.0549° |
| **Argument of Perigee** | 144.2136° |
| **Mean Anomaly** | 215.9805° |
| **Mean Motion** | 15.97751051 rev/day |
| **Orbital Period** | ~90.13 minutes |
| **Revolution at Epoch** | 1,324 |
| **B\* drag term** | 0.00025961 (1/Earth radii) |
| **ṅ (mean motion derivative)** | +0.00116972 rev/day² |

### Perigee Evolution Over Time

| Date (approx.) | Perigee (km) | Apogee (km) | Period (min) | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| ~Mar 31, 2026 (initial catalog) | ~288 | ~324 | 90.64 | russianspaceweb.com |
| ~Apr 29, 2026 (TLE epoch) | ~286 | ~310 | 90.48 | N2YO (older TLE) |
| Late Apr/early May (parsec.ro) | ~279 | ~297 | 90.27 | parsec.ro |
| **Jun 14, 2026 (latest TLE)** | **~274** | **~287** | **90.13** | **N2YO (current TLE)** |

### Orbital Decay Trajectory

- **Perigee decay rate:** ~0.26 km/day (averaged April 29 → June 14)
- **Apogee decay rate:** ~0.50 km/day (same period)
- **SMA decay rate:** ~0.38 km/day
- The orbit is circularizing as it decays (eccentricity dropped from 0.0018431 to 0.0009892 over 46 days).
- The high n-dot value (+0.00117 rev/day²) and elevated B* confirm significant atmospheric drag.

### Predicted Reentry

- **Sonik.space** (computed ~April 29, 2026) predicted reentry around **September 4, 2026**.
- Given ongoing decay, this remains plausible but depends on whether the satellite continues station-keeping maneuvers.

### Constellation Context

RASSVET-3 10 is one of 16 Rassvet-3 satellites launched together on March 23, 2026, as Russia's first operational broadband LEO constellation batch (often called "Russian Starlink"). As of early June 2026:

- **1 satellite lost:** Object 4 (NORAD 68363) reentered ~June 6, 2026, having never maneuvered.
- **6 satellites actively climbing** to higher orbits (gaining 40-50 km).
- **8 satellites (including No. 10) maintaining altitude** via station-keeping against natural decay.
- **1 satellite** (Object 10 / NORAD 68369) is among those maintaining altitude but **not** actively raising orbit — it is performing the minimum maneuvers needed to counteract drag, which explains its continued slow altitude loss despite being operational.

### Key Caveat on Data Sources

- The N2YO **displayed** parameters (293 km perigee, 317.6 km apogee) on the satellite page are from a **cached earlier snapshot** (~late April). The **TLE itself** on that same page has epoch June 14, 2026, and yields the more current values (~274 km perigee).
- Orbital elements change continuously due to atmospheric drag; the June 14 TLE is valid for SGP4 propagation near that epoch only.
